document_metadata.project_name: BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments

aim.intro: The Request for Tender (RFT) is for the BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments to Council for the service of procurement, installation, and integration of specified works. The tender period spans over a duration agreed upon in context.
aim.body: This Tender Evaluation and Probity Plan (TEPP) is the planning and control document in conducting the evaluation of tenders received in response to RFT. It sets out: • processes and principles for evaluating tenders; • individual's responsibilities, including ensuring all work complies with BCC WHS requirements and proper crane usage approvals are obtained from Council before hoisting equipment; • the evaluation schedule based on a detailed program submitted by tenderer to minimize disruption during peak hours of leisure centre operation (6:00am - 9:00pm daily); and • reporting requirements, including making good all disturbed work surfaces as per context.

description_of_requirement: The BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments involve the comprehensive upgrade of existing heating, ventilation and air conditioning systems within a leisure centre's main pool hall. This includes replacing outdated equipment with new reverse cycle water-cooled heat pump chillers to enhance energy efficiency.

The refurbishment work is scheduled around the operational requirements of the Blacktown Leisure Centre, ensuring minimal disruption during peak hours and coordinating deliveries for seamless installation.

General:
• All works must comply with Australian Standards & NSW WHS regulations.
• Licensed Personnel Only
• Minimal Disruption to Leisure Centre Operations during refurbishment work, adhering strictly within the centre's operational hours of 6:00am to 9:00pm daily. Noisy works shall be limited or scheduled outside these times.
• Interruption and Delivery Coordination
• All deliveries for materials required in refurbishment must occur during agreed-upon delivery windows, ensuring no disruptions to the centre's operations.
technical_requirements:
Electrical Installation:
• All equipment supplied and work done shall comply with AS/NZS Wiring Rules, local Electricity Supply Authority requirements.
• Switchboards to conform in all respects with the latest edition of AS 61439. Type test certificate from a qualified testing laboratory required for switchboard manufacturers' submission.
• All cables not enclosed shall be neatly arranged and tied, supported where necessary; power cables terminating at contactors/overloads allowed directly without additional terminals if specified in the design.
• Wiring penetrating a fire wall to use special intumescent fittings as per AS 3000 standards for compliance with local regulations and safety measures.
• Contractor responsible for testing, commissioning including visual inspections of pipework joints; megger tests on circuit terminations; integrity checks on control cabling to ensure electrical continuity without shorts or grounds. Sequence checks required for specified controls functions such as Start/Stop cycles and Chiller Staging.
• All wiring in mechanical services plant room areas must conform with AS 3000 standards, run through conduit, cable trunking on trays to suit application; all external equipment connections using anaconda flexible steel sheathed conduit or Clipsal type Flexible PVC if rated for duty and coated externally.
• All ductwork protruding through walls must be sealed with galvanised steel frames, insulated to prevent thermal losses as per NCC Section J requirements; all materials used in installation of insulation tested against AS 1530 Part 3 standards for early fire hazard properties.
• Disconnect power supply cables from main switchboard into MSSB-1 and reconnect the same back after replacing with new ones to accommodate additional chillers, ensuring correct control status on Control. Supply adequately sized power cables between existing sub-boards for auxiliary equipment including heat recovery circulation pumps.
• Install 7.5kW ABB ACH580 VSD's with respective controls to the new chillers, ensuring correct control and status on Control as per design drawings.
• Supply cable tray, cabling, isolator for exhaust fans in AHUs; install switchgears accommodating additional equipment including relays for BMS interfingcing. Ensure all wiring is neatly arranged with proper supports where necessary and complies with local regulations on sealing ductwork.
• Submit detailed schedule of work to Council immediately after award, based on the council's provided timeline; ensure noisy works are limited within typical working hours or agreed upon by leisner centre's management.

Switchboard Upgrade:
• Provide a plinth with SS Tray under the feet of each closed system.
• Install corrosion coupons for testing every three months in accordance with AS/NZS standards and local Electricity Supply Authority requirements, ensuring switchboard manufacturers have type test certificates from qualified laboratories.
• Provide method statements and SWMS for all high-risk activities
• Coordinate works with other trades and Council representatives
• Verify dimensions and services prior to fabrication/installation
• Test and commission to manufacturer and AS/NZS requirements
• Provide as-built documentation and O&M manuals at completion

Commissioning of Electrical Works:
• All equipment supplied and work done shall comply with AS/NZS standards, S.A.A Wiring Rules, local Electricity Supply Authority requirements.
• Switchboards to conform with AS 61439; submit type test certificate from a qualified testing laboratory for the switchboard manufacturer's submission.
• Ensure programming of work and site management minimizes disruption during leisure centre's opening hours (6:00am-9:00pm daily).
• All wiring penetrating fire walls to be installed using special fire rated intumescent fittings.
• Provide all necessary resources for testing and commissioning, including labor, materials, power, consumables; conduct tests before Council inspections with detailed Test Log sheets (A4 size).
• Visual inspection of pipework and joints for leaks; water-test drains and overflow piping.
• Check all power cabling terminations using megger test each circuit; verify integrity of controls cabling to ensure electrical continuity, free from shorts and grounds.
• Sequence checks of specified control functions (Start, Stop, Chiller Staging) with re-engineering and programming for BMCS integration post refurbishment AHU 2. Allow progressive fine-tuning up to the end of Defects Liability Period without additional cost.
• Label 'Authorised Personnel Only' sections in SCA switchboards; provide LED pilot light indications with individual fault indication for safety controls and lamp test button on all main components.
• All cables not enclosed shall be neatly arranged, tied loosely where necessary, supported neatly. Power cabling terminations to terminal blocks directly or via contactors/over-loads as appropriate; no more than two cables per point except for specified exceptions (e.g., Compressor and Condenser motors).
• Remove external wall louvre before delivery of equipment, remove job generated rubbish offsite.
• Coordinate with centre management regarding access to stocks needed for operation; ensure safe movement through work area by pool staffs during crane lifts. All hoisting and associated works must comply with SafeWork NSW requirements without any additional cost borne by Council or Contractor, except where special approval is given.
• Maintain a minimum 12-month warranty for installation and equipment; include preventative maintenance in the tender to cover routine visits (at least once per month) until end of Defects Liability Period as required by AS 3666, Local Authority or other statutory body.
• Ensure all works are carried out fully complying with proper BCC WHS requirements; provide a cranage strategy and execute it to Council's satisfaction. All equipment must fit in hoisting facilities available before ordering.

Maintenance Schedule for Electrical Systems:
• Immediate submission of detailed work schedule to Council post-award.
• Working hours limited to typical leisure centre operation times or agreed upon with management, especially during noisy works.
• Minimum notice period of 48 hours for electrical shutdowns required by written approval from the council officer in charge and center's management.
• Ensure all equipment supplied complies strictly with AS/NZS standards relevant to mechanical services, air & water schematic drawings, power supply diagrams, AHU specifications, EC fan wiring diagram, etc., as well as any other S.A.A publications and local Electricity Supply Authority requirements.
• Switchboards must comply with AS 61439 standards; provide proof of performance through a type test certificate from an accredited testing laboratory for the switchboard manufacturer's submission.
• All connections to be neat, arranged and supported as per drawings. Power cables directly terminating on contactors/overloads where applicable.
• Installation of 7.5kW ABB ACH580 VSD's for circulating pumps in heat recovery coils with appropriate labeling indicating 'Authorised Personnel Only' and fault indications as per switchboard drawings requirements.
• All cables except those enclosed to be neatly arranged, loosely tied where necessary. Control cables terminating at approved terminal blocks without exceeding two cable terms per point.
• Removal of external wall louvre for equipment delivery and retrieval with proper disposal off-site including redundant equipment as required by the council officer in charge.
• Coordination with center management to ensure safe access, movement through work areas during progressive fine-tuning up to end of Defects Liability Period without additional cost.
• Hoisting and cranage works for lifting heavy items must be carried out fully within council's written approval. No hoisting unless approved by Council with proper planning, including the number of required lifts in line with staged nature of work.
invoicing: Invoices must be itemised and reference purchase order and contract number
Standard 30 day payment terms from receipt of valid tax invoice
Milestone/progress claims to align with agreed program and deliverables
Variations require prior written approval by Council
reporting: Weekly progress report with status, risks, and updated program
Monthly summary including KPI performance and incidents
Final report pack including commissioning results and certificates
Provide as-built drawings, O&M manuals and warranties

performance_monitoring.note: The provided text does not contain specific performance monitoring criteria directly related to a contract's execution but rather outlines various aspects of the mechanical services, particularly focusing on air & water systems and electrical requirements for refurbishments at Blacktown Leisure Centre. The context includes details about testing procedures, warranty periods, maintenance responsibilities, noise control measures during construction, coordination between contractors, as well as specifications regarding the installation of switchboards in compliance with AS 61439 and requirements for a Building Management Control System (BMCS).
items: All wiring penetrating fire walls must use special intumescent fittings.

Contractor to provide all equipment, labour, materials, power, consumables etc. required for testing and commissioning before Council inspections with fully detailed Test Log sheets in A4 size format prior to any tests taking place.

Visual inspection of pipework and joints for leaks; water-test drains and overflow piping for unobstructed drainage, power cabling terminations check, megger test each circuit. Sequence checks of all specified control functions (Start, Stop, Chiller Staging, Cooling & Heating Cycles).
Water flow rates to be balanced within +5%/-0% of scheduled flows; record and submit temperature set points and differentials.

Installation Contractor responsible for all preventative maintenance and servicing during Defects Liability Period, including routine monthly visits as per AS 3666 standards. Maintenance to include rectification faults, emergency service, capacity tests with a minimum of 12 regular monthly visits.
Warranty for installation and equipment is set at a period of no less than 12 months from practical completion date.

Electrical shutdown arrangements must be made in consultation with the leisure centre's management, providing at least 48-hour written notice to avoid disruption during working hours or agreed upon times. No extra costs for such interruptions will be claimed by tenderers.

During construction works within occupied areas: minimize dust and dirt; collect and remove all rubbish daily; ensure noise, vibration control measures are in place as per council best practices to prevent disruption of leisure centre activities. Coordination between HVAC and BMCS contractors is mandatory for integrated system functionality.

All electrical equipment supplied must comply with AS 61439; switchboard manufacturer shall provide a type test certificate from an accredited laboratory, ensuring the design conforms to S.A.A Wiring Rules and local Electricity Supply Authority requirements.

desired_outcomes: Reliable delivery of all materials and equipment as per schedule
Timeliness in completing the work within the agreed timeframe without causing significant disruption to leisnery center's operations during peak hours (6:00am-9:00pm)
High quality installation with minimal damage, ensuring all surfaces and penetrations are made good as per Council requirements
Qualified staff employed for the execution of work in accordance to BCC WHS standards
Effective complaint management system established by Contractor's representative on site at all times during worksite activities

Proper administration and adherence to contractual obligations, including timely payments as per Council's Tender terms for any additional services or materials required due to unforeseen circumstances such as hoisting equipment damage
Efficient handling of redundant equipment with proper recycling protocol in place
purpose_rft: Set out the requirements, terms and evaluation method for the procurement
Identify either a single supplier or a panel arrangement as permitted by legislation
Achieve best value consistent with Council policies and probity

risks.action: FAT; point-to-point I/O test; SAT with witness
Verify duty; commissioning with TAB report

evaluation_schedule.task: Review Testing & Commissioning plan and witness tests
Review maintenance schedules and O&M handover
Review BMCS HLI/point list and integration test records

tender_evaluation.methodology_text: The Evaluation Committee, in accordance with the methodology specified in the RFT and reproduced here, will evaluate all tenders.

required_criteria_table.rationale: Given the high integration risk and commissioning complexity indicated by 'commissioning_complexity' being at a level of 20, it is crucial to allocate more weight towards price as this will ensure that we have enough budget flexibility for potential unforeseen costs. The chosen percentage reflects an emphasis on cost-effectiveness without compromising the other important factors

With 'num_ec_fans' at 8, it is essential to prioritize experience in energy efficiency and cooling systems which are relevant for a service delivery contract involving chillers. This ensures that we select an experienced provider with proven track records in similar projects

The 'has_chillers' flag indicates the need for specialized capability, hence this criterion is weighted higher to ensure competency and expertise are prioritized when selecting a service delivery contractor. This aligns well with our project complexity level of 20

A moderate allocation here ensures that the selected provider has enough financial stability, which is important for managing complex projects and maintaining quality standards without overemphasizing this aspect to an extent where it might compromise other critical factors like experience or capability

Although 'has_quality_requirements' was false in the context provided, a reasonable weight ensures that we do not neglect overall service delivery and quality management which are vital for project success. This is especially important given our safety-critical nature of work (safety_criticality: 3)

Given 'warranty_months' being at a year, it indicates the need to prioritize health and welfare aspects in service delivery. A higher weight ensures that these standards are met without overshadowing other critical factors

evaluation_methodology.overview: The Evaluation Committee, in accordance with the evaluation methodology specified in the RFT and reproduced in this document, will evaluate all tenders. Compliance is generally taken to mean submission of the Tender by the closing date, in accordance with all lodgement instructions and provision of all information requested in the RFT (including any late tender provisions). Compliance criteria are not scored or weighted; depending on the nature, extent and implications of partial or non-compliance, a Tender may be disqualified from further evaluation.

compliance_criteria_table.title: 6.1.1 Compliance Criteria
notes: Compliance criteria are not scored or weighted. Non-compliant tenders may be excluded from further evaluation.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Exceptional
non_price_rating_scale.description: Fully compliant; no risks or weaknesses.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Superior
non_price_rating_scale.description: Sound achievement; minor risks/weaknesses may be acceptable.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Good
non_price_rating_scale.description: Reasonable achievement; errors/risks can be corrected with minimal effort.

non_price_rating_scale.label: Adequate
non_price_rating_scale.description: Minimal achievement; issues possible to correct/overcome.

returnable_scoring.note: See evaluation workbook and schedules for detailed rubrics.

pricing.pricing_model_text:
Pricing for tender evaluation will be normalised; the lowest fee achieves the maximum score (equal to the price weighting). State Government method:
Ps = 200 – (Pc / Pav × 100)
Pn = Ps / 200
Pw = Pn × [Price Weight]
Pc = Total Net Cost, Pav = Average of Total Net Costs.

local_preference_policy.blacktown_lga_discount: 5% discount capped at $50,000 for suppliers located in Blacktown City LGA
local_preference_policy.western_sydney_discount: 2.5% discount capped at $25,000 for suppliers located in Western Sydney councils
definitions: Blacktown City Local supplier: principal place of business (not a PO Box) located within Blacktown City LGA.
Western Sydney Council supplier: principal place of business (not a PO Box) located within a Western Sydney Council LGA.
local_preference_policy.application_note: For any suppliers eligible for local preference, provide the confirming address in the third tab of the tender evaluation spreadsheet.

value_for_money: Assessed on qualitative outcomes + price: quality and fitness for purpose vs whole-of-life costs vs risk.
Consider whole-of-life costs incl. maintenance and warranty obligations
Energy efficiency and control strategy for chillers/AHUs
‘Value for Money’ is assessed on the combined outcomes of qualitative criteria and price.
Consider quality of proposed services against whole-of-life costs and risk (capacity to deliver on-time and on-budget).
pricing.interviews_and_presentation_criteria: Scores may be revised following interviews/presentations of shortlisted tenderers.

model.narrative: Pricing for tender evaluation will be normalised; the lowest fee achieves the maximum score equal to the Price criterion weighting. In accordance with State Government scoring, the Total Net Cost for tender evaluation is normalised and weighted for comparison as follows.
variables: Pc = Total Net Cost calculated for tender evaluation
Pav = Average of all Total Net Costs
notes: The total score each Tenderer receives provides a numeric basis for comparison.
Recommendation on the preferred Tender will be based on scoring comparisons.
The basis of any increase will be calculated for the full term of the contract, including optional extensions.

contract_negotiations: A period of negotiation with the successful Tenderer may arise following completion of the Tender phase and will commence subject to approval by the Director. This period will result in the executed copies of the contractual documentation being completed and will necessitate a combination of meetings comprising formal written obligations to be resolved. The role of the negotiators is to ensure that the negotiation approach is appropriate to the nature of the project, is open and fair, meets the needs of the Tenderer and can be accommodated within the Tenderer’s resources. Negotiations must not result in material changes to the requirements of the Tender. The outcome of the negotiations will be reflected in the final contract and all negotiation discussions and outcomes will be documented.
debriefing: The Evaluation Panel may arrange for the debriefing of any unsuccessful Tenderers who request such a debriefing. The purpose of the debriefing is to provide Tenderers with general comments regarding evaluation of the Tenderer’s submission against the criteria. If undertaken, the debriefing process will be conducted by at least two members of the Evaluation Committee and may be carried out by telephone or letter. The debriefing process will be limited to the unsuccessful Tenderer’s offer. No comparisons will be made with the successful Tender and the debriefing process will not be used to justify the selection of the successful Tender. No scoring details will be provided.
contract_management: The contract will be managed by Manager City Fleet or delegate.
critical_dates: Commencement date in spec (01 Aug 2025) is earlier than policy minimum; adjusted to 07 Oct 2025 (≥ title date + 26 days).
how_to_complete_tepp: 1) Title Page — Replace Tender Number, Name and Term.
2) 1. Aim — Update Tender Name and Term.
3) 2. Description of Requirement — Replace with contract description and main requirements.
4) 2.1 Desired Outcomes — Insert procurement outcomes (value for money, timely delivery, etc.).
5) 2.2 Purpose of RFT — Replace with reasons for this tender (or EOI/Quotation).
… (continue your internal guidance list as needed) …
Update footer to correct Tender Number and Tender Name.

summary: The BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments involve the comprehensive upgrade of existing heating, ventilation and air conditioning systems within a leisure centre's main pool hall. This includes replacing outdated equipment with new reverse cycle water-cooled heat pump chillers to enhance energy efficiency.

The refurbishment work is scheduled around the operational requirements of the Blacktown Leisure Centre, ensuring minimal disruption during peak hours and coordinating deliveries for seamless installation.
Scope includes: Replacement/upgrade of water-cooled heat pump chillers; AHU coil refurbishments; 8 EC plug fans; BMCS integration (e.g., BACnet HLI)

shortlisting: Clearly non-competitive tenders may be excluded with reasons recorded.
alternative_tenders: Alternative tenders will be evaluated in accordance with the RFT evaluation methodology reproduced in this document.
tenderer_presentations: Tenderers may be invited to interview to clarify their tender. No new or additional information will be requested or permitted at this stage (see Section 3.8 – Requests for Clarification). Answers must be factual and unbiased; no personal opinions are to be expressed.
evaluation_report: On completion of the evaluation, results will be documented in a Council Report including:
• a summary of the evaluation method,
• the recommendation of the preferred tender, and
• the rationale used to select the preferred supplier.

The report will highlight key issues for negotiation/ongoing scrutiny, be considered by the Tender Review Committee, and, if approved, be endorsed by all Committee Members and presented to the Ordinary Council Meeting for adoption. The ultimate decision maker is Blacktown City Council’s elected officials.
debriefing_unsuccessful: Debriefing may be offered upon request. Only general comments on the tenderer’s own submission; no comparisons; no scoring details.

compliance_criteria: Comply with SafeWork NSW / Council WHS
Provide PPE for all on-site workers
Provide commissioning plan and evidence of tests per Section 10

required_criteria.name: Methodology
required_criteria.description: Commissioning plan covering: 10.1 General; Provide all equipment, labour, materials, power, consumables, etc. to conduct testing and; commissioning. The Contractor shall carry out his own tests to verify operation and performance; before requesting Council to attend inspections. Submit fully detailed Test Log sheets for inspection; prior to any testing taking place. The Test Log sheets shall be A4 in size and present a clear and; precise record of all tests to be carried out.…; BMCS integration/HLI (BACnet) with IO schedules and point lists; Program includes defects liability / warranty of 12 months; Provision for service/maintenance handover & schedules

required_criteria.name: Quality
required_criteria.description: Quality plan incl. ITPs; as-built docs; performance verification

required_criteria.name: Work Health & Safety
required_criteria.description: Comply with SafeWork NSW / Council WHS; Provide PPE for all on-site workers; Electrical work to AS/NZS 3000

interviews_presentations: Final scores may be revised following interviews/presentations conducted with shortlisted Tenderers, in accordance with Section 6.4 Tenderer Presentations.
shortlisting_alternatives: Where many tenders are received, clearly non-competitive tenders with no reasonable prospect of best value may be excluded from detailed evaluation. Reasons for exclusion must be defensible and recorded in the evaluation report.
